learning_objectives: Name validated feline acute pain scales with rescue thresholds: Feline Grimace Scale (5 action units, max 10, threshold ≥4); UF EPS Short Form (max 12, threshold ≤4); Glasgow Composite Skin Pain Scale (max 20, threshold ≥5)
Name validated canine acute pain scale: Glasgow CMPS-SF (max 24 mobile/20 immobile, threshold ≥6/≥5)
Name validated chronic CMIs: CBPI (11 items, 3 domains) and LOAD (13 items) for dogs; Feline Musculoskeletal Pain Screening Checklist for cats
Describe all 5 Feline Grimace Scale action units: ear positioning, orbital tightening, muzzle tension, whisker position, head position
Explain FAS (Fear, Anxiety, Stress) impact on concurrent pain scale interpretation and required intervention before re-scoring
Apply validated assessment workflow to geriatric cat presenting with behavioral changes
Recognize prey species pain masking behavior from an evolutionary standpoint and apply minimum post-procedural assessment standard
Implement pain as the fourth vital sign through concrete practice-level workflow changes
high_priority_objectives: Name validated feline and canine pain scales with specific rescue threshold scores
Recognize prey species pain masking and apply minimum post-procedural assessment standard
mastery_threshold_note: Fellows must know specific validated tool names, domain structures, and rescue thresholds. Generic descriptions of behavioral assessment without tool names or scores indicate insufficient mastery.
